title: Azure Logic Apps - Managed Connectors: Blob Storage, Service Bus, and Azure Monitor Logs
applies_to: Logic Apps reading or writing to Azure Blob Storage
Logic Apps sending or consuming Azure Service Bus messages
Logic Apps running KQL queries against Log Analytics workspaces

confirmed_facts.fact: Blob Storage V2 actions use double encodeURIComponent for both the storage account name and the folder/file path in path segments. Single encoding is insufficient and returns a connector error.
Blob content retrieved from the connector is returned as $content (base64-encoded) and $content-type. To get the raw text: @base64ToString(body('Get_Blob_Content')['$content']).
The Blob Storage managed connector has a 50 MB per-file limit without chunking. Enable chunked transfer in action settings to support files up to 1 GB.
Service Bus ContentData must be base64-encoded before sending. The connector does not auto-encode. Use @base64(body('Compose_Message')) to encode.

To receive Service Bus messages, the trigger uses a polling pattern with a recurrence sub-block. splitOn: '@triggerBody()' processes one message per workflow run. Without splitOn, the trigger fires with an array.
The Service Bus LockToken from the trigger body is required for peek-lock complete/abandon/dead-letter operations. Access it as @triggerBody()?['LockToken'].
The Azure Monitor Logs connector (key: azuremonitorlogs) runs KQL queries via POST /queryData. The response value array is at @body('action')?['value']. Max 500,000 rows. 110-second timeout.

The Log Analytics query timerange field: 'Set in query' delegates time filtering to the KQL query itself (requires a TimeGenerated filter). Alternatively use preset strings like 'Last 24 hours' or 'Last 7 days'.

The Azure Blob Storage connector key in $connections is 'azureblob'. The managed API ARM ID uses lowercase: .../managedApis/azureblob. Service Bus uses 'servicebus' / .../managedApis/servicebus. Azure Monitor Logs uses 'azuremonitorlogs' / .../managedApis/azuremonitorlogs.

blob_storage_patterns.note: Double encodeURIComponent is required for V2 blob paths. 'AccountNameFromSettings' is a connector placeholder — parameterize the account name for deployments.

send_message.note: ContentData must be base64-encoded. Label is the message subject. UserProperties carries custom metadata.

complete_message_peek_lock.note: For topics: replace queue path with '<topic>/subscriptions/<subscription>/messages/head'.

body.query:
SecurityAlert
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where AlertSeverity == 'High'
| summarize count() by AlertName
| order by count_ desc

key_facts: timerange: 'Set in query' requires a TimeGenerated filter in the KQL. Preset values: 'Last 24 hours', 'Last 7 days', 'Last 30 days'.
Connector name in $connections may be 'azuremonitorlogs' or 'azuremonitorlogs_1' — check your actual connection.
Auth: Managed Identity recommended. The Logic App's MSI must have Log Analytics Reader role on the workspace.
Each row in the response value array is a JSON object with column names as keys.

implementation_notes: Blob V2 paths require double encodeURIComponent — this is a connector-level requirement. Single encoding causes 404 or 400 even with a correct path.
Blob content is returned as base64. Always decode with @base64ToString(...) before processing as text or JSON.
Service Bus messages must be base64-encoded before sending. The connector does not encode automatically.
For Service Bus peek-lock receive, always add a Complete (or Abandon/Dead-letter) action after processing. Messages not completed within the lock timeout are re-queued.
Service Bus topics: path uses the pattern /<topic>/subscriptions/<subscription>/messages/head. Replace 'my-queue' in the queue pattern with this path.
Log Analytics queries should include | take N to cap results and avoid timeout. The connector timeout is 110 seconds regardless of row count.
For Log Analytics, use 'Set in query' timerange + explicit TimeGenerated filter in KQL. This gives precise control. Preset timerange values are coarse and ignore time-of-day.
All three connectors support MSI authentication — recommended over credential-based connections for automated deployments.
common_failures: Blob: single encodeURIComponent on path — connector returns 404 even for an existing blob.
Blob: accessing body('Get_Blob_Content') directly as text — it is base64. Must use @base64ToString(body('Get_Blob_Content')['$content']).
Service Bus: ContentData not base64-encoded before send — message body is corrupted on the receiving end.
Service Bus: missing splitOn on trigger — fires once with a batch of messages instead of once per message.
Service Bus: LockToken not passed in Complete action — message re-queues after lock timeout even though it was processed.
Log Analytics: query without TimeGenerated filter when timerange is 'Set in query' — returns all historical data and times out.
Log Analytics: connector timeout (110s) hit before query returns — reduce result set size with | take or | summarize.
